summary: Reconcile the README + AGENTS.md's stale CLI/posture claims (retired `import`/`diff` verbs, hand-authored-TOML requirements) against 20-surface's ratified surface — a targeted fix, not a rewrite

per.section: The front door — the README is the landing page

edit.description: Three stale claims verified against the trunk-head binary (`temper --help`): (1) README.md:77 (Status) lists the verb set as `import, check, schema, diff, emit, install, bundle` — `import` and `diff` were retired by the six-noun demolition; the real surface is init / check / schema / emit / guard / install / bundle / explain. Correct the list. (2) README.md:42 ('A typed surface, not a rule pile') says '`import` scans the whole harness' — `import` is no longer a verb; the scan now lives in `init` and in `check --harness`'s internal one-shot import. Re-ground on `init`. (3) README.md:46 ('Requirements you declare. In a `temper.toml` …') describes the hand-authored-TOML posture the sole-producer ruling rejects — 20-surface: emit is the sole lock producer, the postures are prose media never config dialects ((inplace-lock-producer) RESOLVED). Re-ground the requirements story in the ratified model. Scope honesty: this is a *reconcile* of the stale claims only — 55-offering's larger launch obligations (logo, badge polish, ~800-word shape) ride the v0.1 offering work, NOT this entry. Do not rewrite prose that is already accurate.

AGENTS.md:27-29 orients contributors with '`import.rs`/`drift.rs` (the surface and its three-state drift engine)' — `import` is a retired verb and the three-state merge (desired/last-applied/real) was deleted in the floor wave (drift collapsed to one authored direction + two freshness facts, 20-surface 'Drift — one direction, two freshness facts'). Update the one-line orientation so it names the current surface (init/emit; drift.rs's freshness model), not the demolished one. Lower stakes than the README — contributor docs — but fix it in the same pass while the files are open. Touch only the stale orientation line; leave the rest intact.

acceptance: README.md and AGENTS.md name only the real trunk-head verbs (init/check/schema/emit/guard/install/bundle/explain) and describe the sole-producer / init-on-ramp posture; no reference to `import` or `diff` as a verb, no hand-authored-TOML requirements claim, no three-state drift engine survives.
notes: Preflight for the public flip (inbox 2026-07-05) — the repo flips public soon and the front door describes the pre-demolition product. Both files sit outside build's fence (^(\.claude|docs|specs)/), so this is fileable build work. Disjoint from PACKAGING-CHANNELS (package.json + release.yml). A reconcile, not the 55-offering launch rewrite.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm packages, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org + scope — John is setting up @dtmd, name probably @dtmd/temper; marketplace publish; signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end; channel 1 (SDK publish) also rides the SDK-primary foundation

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution, 'install is two placements').

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery'). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering it. Scoped names per the resolved `(project-name)` fork — John is provisioning the `@dtmd` scope (name probably `@dtmd/temper`); use the confirmed scope, not the placeholder `@temper`. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the SDK authoring-face manifest — channel 1's core + provider module).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (core + provider module, floors as exported clause arrays); ch2 = engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human creds; scope in motion (@dtmd).
